For latency and throughput percentiles in the inference engine — a streaming alternative to a bucketed histogram.

crates.name: sovereign-p2c-balance
crates.description: Power-of-two-choices (P2C) load balancing: pick two backends at random and send the request to the less-loaded one, which provably keeps the maximum load far lower than random placement while using only O(1) state and no global coordination. Weight-aware (compares in-flight per unit capacity) for routing across heterogeneous compute backends — distinct from weighted round-robin and consistent hashing.

crates.name: sovereign-paged-kv
crates.description: Paged KV-cache memory management (PagedAttention-style): a fixed-size block pool with per-sequence page tables, on-demand block allocation as tokens append, reference-counted copy-on-write so forked sequences share their common prefix blocks, and out-of-memory reporting. The allocator behind non-fragmenting KV-cache serving.

crates.name: sovereign-pagerank
crates.description: PageRank over a directed graph by power iteration, with damping and proper dangling-node handling: rank nodes by their link importance until the scores converge. For ranking documents or entities in a knowledge graph (GraphRAG), citation analysis, and any 'who is important here' question over a link structure.

crates.name: sovereign-pcie-advisor
crates.description: Emit the recommended ProArt X870E-Creator PCIe layout and validate a proposed one against the lane-sharing trap (E0027/E0028), from the single-source-of-truth sovereign-pcie-topology slot map. Catches populating PCIEX16_2 + M.2_2 together before it silently halves a GPU's bandwidth.

crates.name: sovereign-pcie-topology
crates.description: M003 — ASUS ProArt X870E-Creator PCIe topology: the 6-slot map, the lane-sharing trap (PCIEX16_2 shares lanes with M.2_2), and the recommended layout (Blackwell x8 + 4090 x8 + M.2_1 x4 + chipset NVMe). Detects lane-sharing conflicts before they bite.

crates.name: sovereign-perplexity
crates.description: Language-model evaluation by perplexity: scores a model's per-token log-likelihood on a reference token sequence and reports cross-entropy and perplexity. The standard intrinsic LM metric, used to compare models or quantization levels. Composes sovereign-decoder-stack (its Clone-able model is scored without mutating the original).

crates.name: sovereign-pii-redact
crates.description: Detect and redact personally identifiable information from text: emails, US SSNs, IPv4 addresses, and credit-card numbers (validated with the Luhn checksum to cut false positives). Returns typed detections with spans and a redacted copy — a privacy gate for logging, telemetry, and anything leaving the device.

crates.name: sovereign-policy-input
crates.description: E0473/E0474/E0475 — the Policy Fabric decision contract: the 7 policy questions, the 10-field intent-based policy input (subject/action/resource/intent/profile/risk/model-provider/context-sensitivity/side-effect-class/user-approval), and the 9 memory sensitivity classes. The schema a policy engine (OPA/Cedar/OpenFGA) decides over.

crates.name: sovereign-policy-questions
crates.description: M049 Policy Fabric — 7-policy-question runtime surface per E0473 + F04155-F04156 + dump 14988-15000. Doctrine per E0474: 'Can subject do action on object FOR THIS INTENT UNDER THIS PROFILE?' That is sovereignty (verbatim dump 15040).

crates.name: sovereign-precision-profile
crates.description: A declarative, opt-in/opt-out precision plan for the decoder stack: a serde-able profile that maps each layer (and named high-precision projections) to an execution Precision (f32 / ternary / NVFP4 / INT8-VNNI) plus per-tier AVX-512 exploitation flags, with named presets and an f32 default that opts out of all quantization. Nothing is hardcoded — the runtime resolves precision per layer from the profile. Composes sovereign-linear.

crates.name: sovereign-prefix-cache
crates.description: Token-prefix cache (RadixAttention-style): index cached token sequences in a trie so a new request can reuse the longest already-computed prefix instead of recomputing shared prompt tokens. Reports the matched length and the cached value to resume from — the data structure behind prefix KV-cache sharing in batched serving.

crates.name: sovereign-pressure-reactions
crates.description: E0431 / M00760 — the 5 adaptive-intelligence reactions that turn live OS pressure (PSI 6-axis) + hardware idleness into scheduler actions. Observability-as-control-input (M013): pressure SENSED → action prescribed. Consumes sovereign-pressure-sensors + sovereign-hardware-load-sample.

crates.name: sovereign-pressure-sensors
crates.description: M045 Pressure-As-Sensation — 6-axis pressure model (CPU / Memory / IO / GPU / Human-attention / Cost) per E0430 + M00759 dump 13636-13660. PSI (system) + DCGM (GPU) + runtime (cost + attention). Drives M046 6-stage adaptation Stage 1-3 (prompt/memory/workflow).

crates.name: sovereign-product-quantization
crates.description: Product quantization for compact vector storage and fast approximate distance: split each vector into m subspaces, learn a k-means codebook per subspace, and encode every vector as m small codes. Approximate squared distance to a query is then a sum of precomputed per-subspace lookups. Composes sovereign-kmeans; for memory-frugal embedding indexes.

crates.name: sovereign-profile-bundles
crates.description: M042 4 canonical profile bundles per M00710 + R07118-R07132 dump 12448-12473: private / careful / fast / sovereign. Each carries a fixed feature set.

crates.name: sovereign-prompt-compress
crates.description: Selective context compression: drop the most predictable (low-surprisal) tokens and keep the informative ones to fit a token budget, preserving order — the LLMLingua approach to cutting long-context cost. Works from per-token log-probabilities or an entropy/budget target.

crates.name: sovereign-prompt-history-ring
crates.description: Cockpit prompt history ring — bounded FIFO of operator-typed prompts the cockpit surfaces via ↑-arrow recall. Capacity 500; dedups consecutive identical prompts; tracks an active cursor for ↑/↓ navigation.

crates.name: sovereign-prompt-rationale
crates.description: Runtime prompt-dispatch rationale envelope — every model dispatch carries (provider, template_name, bundle, mode, doctrine_tag) explaining which router decision produced it. The cockpit surfaces it next to every model output for transparency.

crates.name: sovereign-prompt-template-registry
crates.description: Runtime prompt-template registry — operator-curated reusable prompts with named variable slots ({{name}}) and rendering. Each template carries (name, body, declared variables, allowed profile bundles, allowed modes). Composes with sovereign-execution-mode-registry + sovereign-profile-bundles for context-gated availability.

crates.name: sovereign-provider-catalog
crates.description: Runtime model-provider catalog — declared inference providers (local-ollama / local-vllm / cloud-anthropic / cloud-openai / cloud-google / mock). Each entry carries (label, endpoint, locality, requires-api-key, allowed_bundles). The router picks among Online + bundle-permitted providers.

crates.name: sovereign-quant-block
crates.description: A precision-selectable transformer decoder block: same pre-norm structure (RMSNorm -> QKV -> RoPE -> causal self-attention -> output proj -> residual -> RMSNorm -> SwiGLU -> residual) as the reference block, but every projection runs through a precision-generic Linear (f32 / ternary / NVFP4). This is quantized transformer inference made real — the block that actually executes on the low-bit kernels. Composes sovereign-linear, sovereign-rmsnorm, sovereign-rope, sovereign-attention, and sovereign-ffn.

crates.name: sovereign-quant-calibration
crates.description: Per-layer quantization calibration: measures the output error a weight matrix incurs under each precision (f32 / ternary / NVFP4) over representative inputs, and recommends the lowest-bit precision that stays within an error budget. The mixed-precision layer-assignment decision a sovereign runtime makes to push as much compute as possible to the cheap kernels without breaking accuracy. Composes sovereign-linear.

crates.name: sovereign-quant-llm
crates.description: The quantized text-to-text runtime: binds the byte-level BPE tokenizer to the mixed-precision QuantModel so a prompt string runs through a heterogeneous f32/ternary/NVFP4 multi-head decoder and back to text. The low-precision counterpart to sovereign-llm, and the true end-to-end realization of local quantized inference. Composes sovereign-tokenizer and sovereign-quant-model.

crates.name: sovereign-quant-model
crates.description: A complete mixed-precision decoder-only model: token embedding -> a heterogeneous LayerStack (f32 / ternary / NVFP4, single- or multi-head per layer) -> final RMSNorm -> output head -> sampler, with prompt ingestion, autoregressive and constrained generation. The quantized counterpart to the f32 decoder-stack, realizing per-layer mixed precision end to end. Composes sovereign-decoder-layer, sovereign-rmsnorm, sovereign-sampler, and sovereign-logit-mask.

crates.name: sovereign-rank-fusion
crates.description: Rank fusion for hybrid retrieval: combine several ranked result lists into one with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (rank-based, score-free) or weighted score fusion (min-max normalized). The standard way to merge a lexical BM25 ranking with a semantic embedding ranking into a single hybrid result.

crates.name: sovereign-rate-limit
crates.description: A token-bucket rate limiter for request throttling: refills at a steady rate up to a capacity (burst), and each request consumes tokens, allowing bursts while bounding the sustained rate. Time is injected (now_ms) so the limiter is fully deterministic and testable. Throttles request frequency, distinct from token-meter's token-budget counting.

crates.name: sovereign-readability
crates.description: Readability scoring: Flesch Reading Ease, Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level, and the Automated Readability Index over text, with a heuristic syllable counter. For checking that generated text matches a target audience's reading level. Composes sovereign-sentence-split for sentence counting.

crates.name: sovereign-regex-constrain
crates.description: Regex-constrained decoding: given a compiled pattern, the text generated so far, and the token vocabulary, produce a LogitMask that allows only the tokens which keep the pattern viable — so a model's output is forced onto a format. Composes sovereign-regex-nfa and sovereign-logit-mask.

crates.name: sovereign-regex-nfa
crates.description: A small regex engine via Thompson NFA construction and simulation: matches a useful subset (literals, ., char classes, *, +, ?, alternation, groups) and — the point — supports incremental stepping over the live state set, so it can drive constrained decoding by saying which next characters a pattern still permits.

crates.name: sovereign-rendezvous-hash
crates.description: Rendezvous (highest-random-weight) hashing: assign each key to the node scoring highest for it, so adding or removing a node only reassigns that node's share of keys and nothing else moves. Weight-aware for heterogeneous capacity, and select_k gives a stable ordered fallback list for replication — distinct from consistent-hash rings (no virtual nodes needed) and load-based balancing.

crates.name: sovereign-repetition-penalty
crates.description: Soft anti-repetition sampling penalties applied to logits from the generated token history: CTRL-style repetition penalty (scale), OpenAI-style frequency penalty (subtract per occurrence) and presence penalty (subtract once if seen). Discourages loops and verbatim repeats without hard-banning tokens — distinct from no-repeat-ngram and logit masking.

crates.name: sovereign-replay-bookmark-set
crates.description: Replay bookmark set — operator-named anchor points within a ConversationThread the cockpit can jump to during a Replay session. Each bookmark declares (label, thread_id, turn_index, color_tag, note). Composes sovereign-conversation-thread + sovereign-replay-cursor.

crates.name: sovereign-replay-cursor
crates.description: Runtime replay cursor — walks a ConversationThread for re-execution under ExecutionMode::Replay. Tracks (current_turn_index, playback_state, breakpoint_role) so the cockpit can step / pause / resume / jump. Companion to sovereign-conversation-thread + sovereign-execution-mode-registry.

crates.name: sovereign-replay-export-bundle
crates.description: Exportable replay bundle — packs (ConversationThread + ReplayCursor + BookmarkSet) into one envelope so the operator can hand a debug session to a colleague. Cross-validates that the cursor + bookmarks reference the bundled thread.

crates.name: sovereign-replay-ledger
crates.description: M012 storage & replay plane — an append-only, hash-chained replay ledger: each entry chains the previous entry's hash, so the whole log is tamper-evident and replayable. The integrity-backed log the replay cursor/bookmark crates navigate over.

crates.name: sovereign-replay-playback-rate
crates.description: Cockpit replay playback rate — discrete operator-selectable rates (0.25x / 0.5x / 1x / 2x / 4x / 8x). The replay cursor advances at the multiplied wall-time intervals between turns. Pure UX; replay correctness owned by selfdef-replay-source-authority.

crates.name: sovereign-rerank
crates.description: A second-stage reranker for retrieval: reorders candidate passages by how many distinct query terms each one covers (not just raw frequency), so a passage touching more of the query's concepts outranks one that merely repeats a single term. The precision pass that follows a cheap first-stage retriever.

crates.name: sovereign-reservoir
crates.description: Reservoir sampling (Algorithm R): keep a uniform random sample of k items from a stream of unknown, unbounded length in O(k) space — for sampling logs, traces, or generations without buffering them all. Seeded with a built-in splitmix64 RNG for fully deterministic, serializable sampling.

crates.name: sovereign-resource-control
crates.description: E0429 / M00756 — systemd resource-control profiles (CPUWeight / MemoryMax / IOWeight / TasksMax / slices+scopes) for the 5 operator-named service boundaries (oracle / scout / sandbox / eval / gateway). Emits deployable systemd drop-in directives: how 'profiles become real OS behavior'.

crates.name: sovereign-retrieval
crates.description: Lexical retrieval for grounding the agent: a deterministic document store that ranks documents by term-overlap with a query, plus a RagResponder that retrieves the top-k documents for each prompt and injects them as context before generation. Retrieval-augmented generation for the agent loop. Composes sovereign-agent-loop.

crates.name: sovereign-retrieval-metrics
crates.description: Information-retrieval evaluation metrics: Precision@k, Recall@k, F1, Mean Reciprocal Rank, Average Precision / MAP, and (normalized) Discounted Cumulative Gain. For measuring and comparing the retrieval stack — BM25, embeddings, rank fusion, MMR — against relevance judgments.

crates.name: sovereign-retry
crates.description: Retry with exponential backoff and deterministic jitter: computes the delay schedule (base * multiplier^attempt, capped), decides whether to retry, and runs a fallible operation with an injectable sleep so the policy is fully testable. The resilience primitive for an agent calling flaky tools or networks.

crates.name: sovereign-rmsnorm
crates.description: RMS normalization: rescales an activation vector by the reciprocal of its root-mean-square and a learned per-channel gain, the pre-norm every Llama-style transformer block applies before attention and the FFN. Cheaper than LayerNorm (no mean subtraction) and scale-invariant by construction.

crates.name: sovereign-roaring-bitmap
crates.description: Compressed bitmap of 32-bit integers, roaring-style: values are split into 16-bit chunks, each stored as a sorted array when sparse and a dense bitset when full, so a set of document ids costs far less than a plain bitset yet still does union, intersection, and difference in near-linear time. For combining posting lists, doc-id sets, and feature flags in retrieval and filtering.

crates.name: sovereign-rolling-hash
crates.description: Polynomial rolling hash with O(1) window updates: Rabin-Karp substring search that finds all occurrences in O(n+m), and content-defined chunking that places chunk boundaries where the rolling hash hits a mask — so an edit only re-chunks locally instead of shifting every boundary. For fast scanning and shift-resistant chunking of ingested text.

crates.name: sovereign-rope
crates.description: Rotary position embeddings (RoPE): rotates each adjacent pair of a query/key vector by a position-dependent angle, so absolute positions are injected as orthogonal rotations and attention scores depend only on relative position. The position-encoding stage that sits in front of the attention kernel.

crates.name: sovereign-rope-scaling
crates.description: RoPE context-length scaling: linear position interpolation, NTK-aware base scaling, and dynamic-NTK for running a rotary-embedding model past its trained context window. Computes the effective position and per-dimension inverse frequencies under each method, the position-side counterpart to the base rope rotation.

crates.name: sovereign-router-7axis
crates.description: M042 7-axis routing brain per F03527-F03533 + R07039-R07045 + dump 12219-12225. NadirClaw doctrine 'many prompts do not deserve the expensive model'. Seven axes: simple/complex · private/public · safe/risky · coding/research/gui · local/cloud · fast/careful · cheap/oracle. Routes incoming tasks to the cheapest viable model in the M075 SRP topology.

crates.name: sovereign-routing-decision-log
crates.description: Runtime router decision log — append-only record of every routing call (selected ProviderId + reason + bundle + mode + ms-elapsed). The replay engine recreates routing from this stream; the cockpit shows it as a 'router timeline' for transparency.

crates.name: sovereign-routing-preference
crates.description: Runtime router preferences — operator-curated weights for the routing decision (prefer-local / prefer-fast / prefer-cheap / prefer-quality / prefer-privacy) per BundleName. The router blends weights to pick among eligible providers.

crates.name: sovereign-running-stats
crates.description: Online statistics for runtime telemetry: Welford's numerically-stable streaming mean/variance/std-dev with min/max, plus an exponential moving average — for tracking latency, tokens-per-second, and acceptance rates without storing every sample.

crates.name: sovereign-runtime-reactions
crates.description: E0472 / M00821 — Telemetry As Control: the 6 real-time runtime-telemetry reaction rules (cost spike / tool failure repeats / hallucination pattern / low memory retrieval quality / GPU pressure high / human gates too frequent). The runtime-level complement to sovereign-pressure-reactions (E0431, OS pressure).

crates.name: sovereign-safetensors-loader
crates.description: Loads a Llama-family model from HuggingFace safetensors (+ config.json) into the runtime's existing multi-head QuantModel — dense f32, dequantizing bf16/f16 on load and permuting HF rotate-half q/k into the runtime's interleaved-RoPE layout. Turns the from-scratch inference stack from 'random weights → gibberish' into 'give it a real model'. GGUF-Q dequant + a real tokenizer bridge are named follow-ups.

crates.name: sovereign-sampler
crates.description: Token sampling engine: turns a logit row into a next-token id through temperature scaling, top-k / top-p (nucleus) / min-p truncation, and repetition penalty, then draws from the resulting distribution with a seeded RNG for fully reproducible decoding. The output stage of the decode loop that sits behind the attention/logits head.

crates.name: sovereign-sandbox-profile
crates.description: E0461 / M00804 — the 8 container/sandbox-fabric profiles (read-only-repo / write-workspace / network-denied / network-docs-only / gpu-scout / no-gpu / vm-isolated / vfio-4090), grouped by the dimension each controls (filesystem / network / gpu / isolation).

crates.name: sovereign-save-state
crates.description: E0451 — ZFS + CRIU together: the 5-layer agent save-state (ZFS snapshot + CRIU checkpoint + replay log + memory record + profile state). A true save-state requires all five; this fixes the layers + the completeness gate.

crates.name: sovereign-secret-scan
crates.description: Detect leaked secrets in text: known-prefix credentials (AWS access keys, GitHub/Slack tokens, private-key headers) plus a high-Shannon-entropy heuristic for unrecognized API keys. Returns typed findings with spans and a redacted copy — a guard against credentials leaking into logs, prompts, or anything sent off the device.

crates.name: sovereign-segment-tree
crates.description: Segment tree with lazy propagation: apply an add to a whole range and query the sum, minimum, or maximum of any range, both in O(log n). Lazy tags defer pending updates down the tree so range-update stays logarithmic instead of linear — strictly more powerful than a Fenwick/BIT (which does point-update, prefix-query). For windowed aggregates, interval accounting, and range allocation.

crates.name: sovereign-self-consistency
crates.description: Self-consistency decoding: draw several answers (varying the seed), normalize them, and return the majority vote with its agreement fraction — trading extra compute for reliability on reasoning tasks. Generic over any answer generator, so it wraps the real runtime or a scripted one.

crates.name: sovereign-semantic-cache
crates.description: A semantic completion cache: stores completions keyed by their prompt's embedding and returns a cached one for any new prompt whose embedding is within a cosine-similarity threshold — so near-duplicate requests (paraphrases, typos) hit the cache, not just exact repeats. The fuzzy counterpart to the exact-match completion cache. Composes sovereign-embed.

crates.name: sovereign-semantic-checkpoint
crates.description: E0450 — Semantic Checkpoints: raw process checkpoint is not enough; an agent checkpoint needs semantic state. The 10 fields (container/process + filesystem snapshot + workflow node + branch state + open tool futures + memory refs + risk state + cost-so-far + expected next action + human gate state) — 'continuity with meaning'.

crates.name: sovereign-semantic-chunk
crates.description: Semantic chunking: group consecutive sentences into chunks, cutting where the embedding similarity between neighbouring sentences drops — so each chunk is topically coherent, unlike fixed-size character chunking. Threshold and percentile-breakpoint variants for retrieval ingestion.

crates.name: sovereign-semver
crates.description: Semantic-version parsing, ordering, and caret-compatibility: parse MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH (with optional prerelease/build), compare per semver precedence (prerelease < release), and check whether one version satisfies another under ^ rules. For validating the crates' schema versions and gating compatibility.

crates.name: sovereign-sentence-split
crates.description: Sentence segmentation: split text into sentences at ./!/? boundaries while avoiding false breaks on common abbreviations (Mr., Dr., e.g.), decimal numbers (3.14), and ellipses (...). A rule-based splitter that feeds sentence-level pipelines like semantic chunking and summarization.

crates.name: sovereign-serve
crates.description: A cost-aware serving orchestrator: for each request it checks the completion cache (a hit costs nothing), estimates complexity for routing, enforces the token budget before generating, runs the model, then records usage and caches the result. The decision path that ties the complexity, cache, and token-meter crates into one $0-aware serve() call. Composes sovereign-complexity, sovereign-completion-cache, and sovereign-token-meter.

crates.name: sovereign-significance
crates.description: Statistical significance for evaluation: percentile bootstrap confidence intervals for a metric, a paired bootstrap test for whether one system beats another on the same examples, and McNemar's test for comparing two classifiers on paired decisions. So 'A scored higher than B' comes with a confidence, not just a number.

crates.name: sovereign-simd
crates.description: The sanctioned SIMD carve-out: the ONE crate permitted `unsafe` (per operator decision) so the DispatchPath scaffolding gets real AVX-512 intrinsics. Every kernel is a safe wrapper that runtime-detects the CPU and dispatches to an AVX-512 path when present, with a scalar reference fallback that is the source of truth — SIMD is proven equal to scalar by tests.

crates.name: sovereign-simhash
crates.description: SimHash near-duplicate fingerprinting: collapse a document's features into a single 64-bit signature by random-hyperplane projection, so similarity is read off the Hamming distance between fingerprints — near-duplicates share most bits. A compact single-word alternative to MinHash for fast dedup and clustering by cosine-like similarity.

crates.name: sovereign-six-pillars
crates.description: M042 6 foundational pillars per R06971-R06986 + dump 12110-12126. The thesis: 'Breakthrough is not one model. Breakthrough is the harness / runtime / workflow / router / memory / evals / hardware-aware execution substrate.'

crates.name: sovereign-skill-library
crates.description: M016 learning without retraining — a Voyager-style skill library: reusable procedural skills (steps + tags) with per-skill use/success tracking and success-weighted retrieval. The agent improves by accumulating + reusing high-success skills, not by touching weights.

crates.name: sovereign-space-saving
crates.description: Space-Saving (Stream-Summary) frequent-items algorithm: keeps exactly k counters and, on an untracked item, evicts the current minimum and inherits its count plus a recorded over-estimate, guaranteeing every item more frequent than N/k is found with a bounded error. Deterministic and hash-free — distinct from a probabilistic Count-Min sketch — for exact-ish top-k over an unbounded stream in fixed memory.

crates.name: sovereign-spec-decode
crates.description: Speculative decoding verification (DFlash family) — greedy draft-then-verify accept rule: accept the longest draft prefix matching the target's greedy tokens, then emit one corrected/bonus token. Pure-Rust reference; the acceleration primitive the AVX++ dump flags as DFlash (3x faster code).

crates.name: sovereign-speculative
crates.description: Speculative decoding over real models: a cheap draft model proposes K tokens, the target model verifies them, and the longest matching prefix (plus one free corrected/bonus token) is committed each round. Greedy verification is lossless — the output is identical to decoding the target alone, just faster. Composes sovereign-decoder-stack (Clone-able models let the draft propose without committing).

crates.name: sovereign-sprt
crates.description: Wald's sequential probability ratio test for Bernoulli outcomes: accumulate the log-likelihood ratio one observation at a time and stop the moment the evidence crosses an accept-H0 or accept-H1 boundary set by the target error rates. Decides whether a success rate is closer to p0 or p1 with far fewer samples than a fixed-size test — for early-stopping online evaluation and A/B comparisons.

crates.name: sovereign-srp-scheduler
crates.description: M075 SRP work-placement scheduler — Conductor (CPU) / Logic Engine (RTX 4090) / Oracle Core (Blackwell PRO 6000). Maps incoming workload class to physical-hardware SRP role per E0719-E0721 + dump 813-837. Doctrine: 'we map the Single Responsibility Principle (SRP) directly to physical hardware layers' (verbatim dump 813).

crates.name: sovereign-sse-parse
crates.description: Streaming Server-Sent Events parser: feed it response chunks as they arrive and it yields complete events (event/data/id/retry fields, multi-line data joined with newlines), buffering partial events across chunk boundaries. For consuming the SSE wire format that streaming LLM APIs use.

crates.name: sovereign-stop-sequence
crates.description: Stop-sequence detection for generation control: finds the earliest occurrence of any configured stop string in generated text (to truncate a reply), plus a streaming scanner that detects stops split across token chunks while holding back only the minimum tail. The 'when to stop generating' boundary above the decode loop.

crates.name: sovereign-stream-decode
crates.description: Incremental UTF-8 decoding for token streaming: feed token bytes one chunk at a time and it emits the valid-UTF-8 prefix immediately while buffering any trailing incomplete multi-byte sequence until it completes. The streaming-output boundary that lets a byte-level tokenizer emit text token-by-token without splitting a character.

crates.name: sovereign-suffix-array
crates.description: Suffix array over a byte string with prefix-doubling construction and Kasai LCP: index every suffix once, then count or locate any substring by binary search in O(m log n), and find the longest repeated substring from the LCP array. For detecting degenerate verbatim repetition in generations and fast substring queries over a fixed text.

crates.name: sovereign-suffix-automaton
crates.description: Suffix automaton: the minimal deterministic automaton recognizing every substring of a string, built online in linear time. Answers substring membership in O(pattern), counts the distinct substrings of the text in O(n), and finds the longest common substring with another string in one pass. For substring search, near-duplicate and overlap analysis, and string indexing — distinct from a suffix array.

crates.name: sovereign-symbolic-plan
crates.description: M031 Symbolic Planning plane — a STRIPS/PDDL forward planner (BFS over grounded actions with precondition/add/delete effects) plus plan verification ('prove things before acting'). Facts are a 64-bit set; pure-Rust reference. The bones the dump says give models formal structure.

crates.name: sovereign-task-lifecycle
crates.description: E0548 / E0556 — the Task Lifecycle: the 12 lifecycle steps (Intake … Resume/Archive) and the 9 task states (active/paused/waiting_user/waiting_tool/hibernated/completed/failed/rolled_back/archived) with a validated state machine + resume requirements.

crates.name: sovereign-task-map
crates.description: E0551 — Map (lifecycle step 4): the 4 domain-specific maps (code / research / GUI / OS-admin) and the components each gathers. 'MAP prevents blind action.'

crates.name: sovereign-tdigest
crates.description: t-digest streaming quantile estimator: clusters values into centroids whose size is bounded by a scale function, so resolution is finest at the extremes (p1, p999) where it matters and coarse in the middle. Gives rank-error-bounded quantiles over an unbounded value range with a small fixed footprint, and merges across shards — the Dunning t-digest used in Elasticsearch and friends. Complements relative-error DDSketch with a different accuracy profile.

crates.name: sovereign-telemetry
crates.description: Live hardware-telemetry probe — reads real kernel/vendor telemetry (Linux PSI, /proc/stat, nvidia-smi) and emits a validated PressureSnapshot + LoadSnapshot as JSON. The first runnable binary in the sovereign-os observability lane; exercises the pressure-sensors + hardware-load-sample ingestion end-to-end.

crates.name: sovereign-telemetry-backend
crates.description: Operator-overrideable telemetry sink selection matching the applied profile schema observability.telemetry_sink (prometheus-local / otel / none, SDD-004 Q-013 + SDD-016): token parsing for the SOVEREIGN_TELEMETRY_SINK env var and the --telemetry-sink CLI flag, plus the CLI > env > profile > default resolution precedence.

crates.name: sovereign-text-edit
crates.description: Precise find-and-replace text editing: apply edits that each require their target to occur exactly once (rejecting ambiguous or missing matches), the safe edit model a code-editing agent uses to change a file without clobbering the wrong spot. The apply side of the line-diff.

crates.name: sovereign-text-eval
crates.description: Generation-quality metrics: BLEU (clipped n-gram precision with brevity penalty) and ROUGE-N / ROUGE-L (n-gram and longest-common-subsequence overlap) for scoring generated text against one or more references. The standard kit for evaluating summaries, translations, and completions.

crates.name: sovereign-token-grammar-mask
crates.description: Bridges a character-level context-free grammar to token-level decoding: given the text generated so far, computes the boolean mask of which vocabulary tokens keep a valid parse alive, plus whether end-of-sequence is allowed. The piece that makes grammar-constrained generation work on a real tokenizer — a model emits tokens, not characters — by feeding each candidate token's string through the Earley recognizer with a cheap first-character prefilter. Composes sovereign-cfg-grammar.

crates.name: sovereign-token-healing
crates.description: Token healing for generation: when a prompt ends mid-token, trim the trailing token(s) and constrain the first generated token to be consistent with the removed text, so the model re-chooses a natural boundary instead of being locked into a bad split. Fixes the prompt/completion seam for completion and constrained decoding.

crates.name: sovereign-token-law-deny
crates.description: The NEGATIVE token-law plane: forbid a running model from ever emitting text that contains any of a set of denied substrings (safety/injection/toxicity phrases). A substring ban is not a per-token property — a forbidden phrase can span token boundaries — so this drives an Aho-Corasick automaton incrementally from the committed generation and bans exactly the tokens that would COMPLETE a banned match. safe_token_ids returns the allow-list (all tokens minus the completing ones), the same shape sovereign-token-law-mask's TokenLawPlanes consumes, so it composes with grammar/regex/policy planes. Composes sovereign-aho-corasick.

crates.name: sovereign-token-law-mask
crates.description: The M002 token-law bitset as a decode-time logit mask (SDD-500). Turns the M00117 token_law_combine allow-mask (sovereign-simd::cheats) into a per-token logit transform: a token whose allow-bit is 0 is set to -inf before sampling, so a running model CANNOT emit it. The FIRST real per-token call site of the M002 bit-machine — policy becomes bits, gating generation. A LogitProcessor for sovereign-logit-pipeline; wired into sovereign-decoder-stack's in-repo decode loop (not the external-proxy path, which exposes no logits).

crates.name: sovereign-token-meter
crates.description: Token accounting and cost metering: tracks input/output tokens against optional budgets, enforces limits before a spend, and estimates cost from per-1k prices. The bookkeeping behind the sovereign runtime's $0-target, cost-aware routing — what tells you whether a request fits the budget before you run it.

crates.name: sovereign-tokenizer
crates.description: Byte-level BPE tokenizer: encodes text to token ids by starting from raw bytes and greedily applying the highest-priority merge rule until none remain, and decodes ids back to bytes. The input/output boundary of the decode stack — every byte has an id, so encoding is lossless and round-trips exactly.

crates.name: sovereign-tool-bridge
crates.description: Schema bridge between the two tool-call dialects: converts OpenAI/Anthropic tool_use JSON (sovereign-tool-call-parse) and the [[tool:NAME|ARGS]] convention (sovereign-tool-dispatch) in both directions, and renders a dispatched ToolOutcome back into OpenAI tool_calls or Anthropic tool_use response blocks. The wire-format adapter an OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible daemon needs to run the ReAct loop with tools.

crates.name: sovereign-tool-call-parse
crates.description: Parse OpenAI-style function/tool calls from model output: pull a tool name and its JSON arguments out of a tool_calls array or a bare {name, arguments} object, tolerating fenced or slightly-malformed JSON via json-repair. The JSON-format counterpart to a [[tool:...]] dispatcher, for agentic runtimes.

crates.name: sovereign-tool-catalog
crates.description: Runtime tool catalog — curated registry of cockpit-callable tools (shell / fs-read / fs-write / web-fetch / model-inference / mcp-bridge / replay-control / cli-bridge). Each entry declares side-effect class + minimum execution mode + required profile bundle so the cockpit can gate availability per session context.

crates.name: sovereign-tool-dispatch
crates.description: Tool-call parsing and dispatch: extracts a [[tool:NAME|ARGS]] call from model output and invokes the registered handler for it, returning the result to feed back into the conversation. The live execution layer above the tool catalog — turns generated text into an action and its result.

crates.name: sovereign-tool-invocation-record
crates.description: Runtime per-invocation record for sovereign-tool-catalog tools — each cockpit-issued tool call emits one immutable record (tool, mode, bundle, started_at, completed_at, exit_kind, bytes_out, trace_id). The replay engine reconstructs the cockpit session from this stream.

crates.name: sovereign-toxicity
crates.description: Configurable toxicity / profanity filtering with obfuscation resistance: normalizes leetspeak and spacing before matching a severity-tiered term list in a single Aho-Corasick pass, returning matches and a score. A content-safety gate distinct from prompt-injection, PII, and secret detection. Composes sovereign-aho-corasick.

crates.name: sovereign-trace-context
crates.description: E0112 / M00215 — the runtime trace mapping: trace_id (user request) / span_id (branch step, model call, tool call) / branch_id (deterministic runtime object) / commit_id (accepted transition). Reconstructable per-trace (R02147).

crates.name: sovereign-transformer-block
crates.description: A complete pre-norm transformer decoder block, assembled from the real primitives: RMSNorm -> Q/K/V projection -> RoPE -> causal self-attention -> output projection -> residual -> RMSNorm -> SwiGLU FFN -> residual, with a stateful KV cache for autoregressive decoding. The unit that stacks to form a model. Composes sovereign-rmsnorm, sovereign-rope, sovereign-attention, and sovereign-ffn.

crates.name: sovereign-trie
crates.description: A prefix trie over strings: insert words and query exact membership, prefix existence, and all completions of a prefix (sorted). The autocomplete / prefix-routing primitive — what powers tool-name completion and command matching.

crates.name: sovereign-trinity
crates.description: M066 Trinity Framework — The Pulse / The Weaver / The Auditor. Project boundary per E0645: Pulse + Weaver live in sovereign-os; Auditor implementation lives in selfdef MS044 (this crate carries only the typed-mirror surface).

crates.name: sovereign-trust-boundaries
crates.description: M014 — isolation and trust boundaries: the 4 trust zones (Host Control / Oracle / Scout-Sandbox / Disposable) and the 4-tier tool sandbox ladder A/B/C/D, with the tier→minimum-zone containment rule that keeps untrusted tools off the host.

crates.name: sovereign-typed-state
crates.description: E0557 — the Critical Data Flow Law: 'Text is not the system state. Text is payload inside typed state.' The real task state as 8 typed components (frames/routes/policies/memory_refs/tool_observations/eval_results/commits/traces). Integrates sovereign-trace-context for traces + commits.

crates.name: sovereign-typical-sampling
crates.description: Locally typical sampling (Meister et al.): keep the smallest set of tokens whose surprisal is closest to the distribution's conditional entropy and whose probability sums to a target mass, then sample from those. A truncation method distinct from top-k / top-p / min-p, aimed at more human-like, less degenerate generation.

crates.name: sovereign-ulid
crates.description: ULID generation: 128-bit identifiers as a 48-bit millisecond timestamp plus 80 bits of randomness, encoded in 26 Crockford base32 characters that sort lexicographically by creation time. Monotonic within a millisecond. For request and trace ids that are unique, time-ordered, and URL-safe — a sortable alternative to UUIDs.

crates.name: sovereign-unigram-tokenizer
crates.description: Unigram-LM subword tokenization (the SentencePiece scheme): given a vocabulary of pieces with log-probabilities, segment text into the maximum-likelihood sequence of pieces with a Viterbi-style dynamic program over the piece lattice. A third tokenizer distinct from byte-pair encoding and WordPiece, with single-character fallback so every input is segmentable.

crates.name: sovereign-union-find
crates.description: Disjoint-set union (union-find) with path compression and union by rank: near-constant-time merging and connectivity queries over a fixed set of elements, plus extraction of the connected components. For clustering near-duplicate pairs (e.g. from LSH) into groups, grouping equivalences, and incremental connectivity.

crates.name: sovereign-value-plane
crates.description: M027 Value Plane — 12-axis reward vector + 7-question test + 5-tier Intelligence Dial per E0248-E0257 dump 7731-8121. PRM doctrine: 'PRM proposes value, CPU applies law, Oracle verifies high-stakes commitments' (E0252). Closing rule: 'Intelligence is knowing which thoughts deserve more life' (E0257 dump 8120 verbatim).

crates.name: sovereign-varint
crates.description: LEB128 variable-length integer coding with zigzag for signed values: encode small integers in one byte and large ones in as many as needed, plus delta-encoding helpers for monotonic sequences. For compactly serializing token-id streams, offsets, and posting lists without fixed-width waste.

crates.name: sovereign-vector-clock
crates.description: Causality tracking for distributed events: a vector clock that decides whether one event happened-before another or whether the two are concurrent (the question a scalar timestamp cannot answer), plus a Lamport clock for a cheap total order consistent with causality. For ordering events across replicas, detecting conflicts, and driving CRDT or gossip reconciliation.

crates.name: sovereign-viterbi
crates.description: Viterbi decoding for hidden Markov models: the log-space dynamic program that finds the single most likely hidden-state sequence behind a series of observations in O(T·S^2), with backpointer reconstruction. For sequence labeling, segmentation, and Unigram-LM-style most-probable tokenization.

crates.name: sovereign-vm-channel
crates.description: E0120 / M00222-M00224 — Communication Boundary: the Host↔4090 channels (virtio-vsock / gRPC-over-vsock / unix-socket-proxy / exchange-folder), the 8 message types, and the VM-propose/host-commit invariant (VM output is always a candidate, never committed directly).

crates.name: sovereign-vm-workload
crates.description: E0119 / M00220-M00221 — 4090-as-isolation-boundary workload suitability: the workloads appropriate for the quarantined VM (draft gen / untrusted experiments / browsing / tool planning / vision-OCR / code-exec / dep-installs / speculative patches) vs the tight-GPU-coupling workloads it must NOT run (tensor sharing / KV cooperation / layer-split / ultra-low-latency sync).

crates.name: sovereign-vnni
crates.description: AVX-512 VNNI (VPDPBUSD) INT8 fusion (M074) — the fused 4-way unsigned×signed byte multiply-accumulate into i32, plus an INT8 matvec built on it. Pure-Rust reference whose numerics match the hardware VPDPBUSD path; the INT8 compute kernel beside the ternary (bitlinear) and 4-bit (nvfp4) kernels.

crates.name: sovereign-vptree
crates.description: Vantage-point tree for exact nearest-neighbor search in a metric space: recursively split points by their distance to a chosen vantage point, then answer k-NN and radius queries while pruning whole subtrees via the triangle inequality. Exact (not approximate) over continuous vectors — a metric-space complement to the discrete-edit-distance BK-tree.

crates.name: sovereign-watermark
crates.description: LLM text watermarking (green-list scheme): at each step a context-seeded hash splits the vocabulary into a 'green' set that gets a logit boost, so generated text carries a statistical signature; detection counts green tokens and reports a z-score against the null hypothesis. For provenance — telling whether text came from a watermarked model — without storing the text.

crates.name: sovereign-weighted-reservoir
crates.description: Weighted reservoir sampling (Efraimidis-Spirakis A-Res): keep a size-k sample from an unbounded stream where each item is kept with probability proportional to its weight, in O(k) space and a single pass. For importance-weighted sampling of logs, examples, or generations. Seeded and deterministic.

crates.name: sovereign-wer
crates.description: Word Error Rate and Character Error Rate: the transcription/ASR metric (substitutions + insertions + deletions over reference length) with the full S/I/D breakdown, built on edit-script alignment. For scoring transcription, normalization, and any reference-vs-hypothesis text comparison. Composes sovereign-edit-align.

crates.name: sovereign-whitelabel
crates.description: M081 — whitelabel mechanism: the surface rebrand taxonomy (must-rebrand / should-rebrand / may-leave / must-not-touch), the 4 rendering strategies (template-substitution / file-overlay / package-replacement / build-time-flag), and the 3 lifecycle stages (pre-build / install-time / first-boot).

crates.name: sovereign-wordpiece
crates.description: WordPiece tokenization (the BERT scheme): greedy longest-match subword segmentation against a fixed vocabulary, emitting '##'-prefixed continuation pieces and falling back to an [UNK] token for words it cannot cover. A real tokenizer variant distinct from byte-pair encoding, with detokenization back to text.

crates.name: sovereign-worker-fleet
crates.description: Fleet health summary over many worker status words (M00212). Read-only observability aggregation: worst load/memory/thermal/queue across the fleet, error/flag counts, and a fleet pressure verdict for the scheduler + cockpit.

crates.name: sovereign-worker-status-word
crates.description: E0111 / M00212 — the worker 64-bit status word. Eight byte-fields (load / memory / thermal / queue / error / health / policy_mode / flags) packed into one u64 the engine writes and the scheduler reads. 'Telemetry becomes bits — each worker gets a status word.'

crates.name: sovereign-workflow-graph
crates.description: E0552 — Plan/Compile (lifecycle step 5): the workflow graph. The 8 node types (model-call / tool-call / memory-read / test-run / policy-gate / human-gate / eval / commit) and a DAG of dependency edges, with cycle detection + topological execution order.

crates.name: sovereign-workspace-folder-registry
crates.description: Runtime workspace-folder registry — operator-declared root folders the cockpit + tool catalog scope filesystem operations to. Each folder declares (label, root_path, scope, read_only, max_size_gb). The dispatcher refuses fs-read/write outside any declared folder.

crates.name: sovereign-world-model
crates.description: M030 World Model plane — a learned tabular state/action/transition model: observe transitions, predict the most-likely next state, roll out trajectories, and measure prediction accuracy. Distinct from the symbolic planner (fixed effects); this learns dynamics from data (Dreamer-style).

crates.name: sovereign-xtc-sampler
crates.description: XTC (Exclude Top Choices) sampler: with a set probability, removes the most-probable tokens whenever several already clear a probability threshold, keeping only the least-probable confident option plus the long tail. Boosts diversity and creativity without the incoherence of high temperature — it only fires when there is a safe alternative, so a single forced token is never excluded. Operates as a logit mask with a seeded activation roll.

crates.name: sovereign-zfs-commit-gate
crates.description: M040 ZFS commit-gate 4-stage cycle per M00678 + R06729-R06732 dump 11692-11697. Snapshot → apply patch → test → commit or rollback. Every agent write goes through this gate.

crates.name: sovereign-zfs-dataset-layout
crates.description: M068 — canonical ZFS dataset layout for the tank pool (context / containers / models / logs / snapshots / vault) with per-dataset recordsize, compression and sync properties, plus a drift validator that flags an observed `zfs get` layout against the catalogued canon (E0660-E0664, F05702-F05730).

crates.name: sovereign-zfs-provisioning-plan
crates.description: M068 M01141/M01142 — emit the ordered zpool create + zfs create/set command plan for the canonical tank layout, setting only the properties that differ from the inherited ZFS/pool defaults (recordsize!=128k, compression!=lz4, sync!=standard) so the provisioning script stays minimal and readable.

crates.name: sovereign-zfs-snapshot-policy
crates.description: M068 E0667 — ZFS snapshot retention policy: classify snapshots (pre-commit / daily / weekly / monthly), the catalogued retention windows (365 / 30 / 90 / 365 days, F05731-F05737), and a pure prune planner that keeps anything younger than its window and never prunes an unclassifiable snapshot.
